CREATE TABLE test_types (id INT, type_name VARCHAR(50));
CREATE TABLE type_mapping (internal_type VARCHAR(50), sql_type INT);
SELECT t.id, t.type_name, tm.sql_type FROM test_types t JOIN type_mapping tm ON t.type_name = tm.internal_type WHERE tm.sql_type > 5;
SELECT DISTINCT tm.sql_type FROM type_mapping tm WHERE tm.internal_type IN (SELECT type_name FROM test_types WHERE id < 4);
SELECT t.id, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM type_mapping WHERE sql_type = 1) AS int_count, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM type_mapping WHERE sql_type = 12) AS varchar_count FROM test_types t WHERE t.id = 1;
SELECT MAX(tm.sql_type) AS max_sql_type, MIN(tm.sql_type) AS min_sql_type FROM type_mapping tm GROUP BY tm.internal_type;
WITH type_counts AS (SELECT internal_type, COUNT(*) AS count FROM type_mapping GROUP BY internal_type) SELECT * FROM type_counts WHERE count > 1;
